name: mediasoup WebRTC SFU Library
what_it_does: Open-source WebRTC SFU (Selective Forwarding Unit) library with Node.js API — enabling developers to build scalable real-time communication applications (video conferencing, streaming, broadcasting) as a low-level WebRTC media server library integrated directly into Node.js applications, with a JavaScript API for managing rooms, transports, producers, and consumers.
use_cases: Building custom multi-party video conferencing via mediasoup Node.js API for video chat applications
Implementing WebRTC media routing via mediasoup for live streaming and broadcast
Building screen sharing applications via mediasoup for remote collaboration tools
Creating low-latency audio/video applications via mediasoup for gaming and interactive media
Replacing Janus/Kurento with Node.js-native SFU via mediasoup for modern stack integration
not_for: Teams without Node.js/WebRTC engineering expertise (use Daily.co or Agora for managed WebRTC)
Production deployments without SFU engineering resources (high complexity)
Simple video calling without server-side media control (use WebRTC peer-to-peer)
best_when: A Node.js team needs a fully customizable, embeddable WebRTC SFU in their existing Node.js application — where mediasoup provides low-level media control without a separate media server process.
avoid_when: You need a managed WebRTC cloud, separate media server process architecture, or WebRTC without Node.js.

auth.notes: Library, not a service — no authentication mechanism. Auth implemented at application level. mediasoup runs as Node.js library within your application — no separate process to authenticate against. Application developer is responsible for all auth. WebRTC transport encryption via DTLS/SRTP (automatic). Rust Worker process (mediasoup-worker) spawned internally by Node.js library.

pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers; infrastructure costs only
pricing.notes: Spanish developers (Iñaki Baz Castillo and José Luis Millán). ISC license (permissive). GitHub: versatica/mediasoup (6,700+ stars). Node.js + native Rust/C++ worker. SFU architecture (no transcoding — forwards media selectively). Very high performance. JavaScript/TypeScript API. Rust Worker spawned automatically. Capabilities: VP8, VP9, H264, AV1 video codecs; Opus, PCMU, PCMA audio codecs; simulcast, SVC. Used by: numerous video conferencing startups. No managed hosting — pure library. mediasoup-demo: reference implementation. mediasoup-client (browser-side library). Community on GitHub and Discord.

performance.rate_limits: No limits — bounded by hardware; scales vertically per CPU core

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Library (not API service) — Node.js integration. ISC open source. No REST API. Rust Worker process. No auth (application responsibility). DTLS/SRTP media encryption automatic. User-controlled data. Excellent documentation. High expertise required.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Router/transport/producer/consumer objects are in-memory — reads idempotent by ID. Creation operations: check for existing objects before creating. WebRTC connections are stateful.
known_agent_gotchas: NOT a service — mediasoup is a Node.js library that you embed in your application, not call via API
Rust Worker binary (mediasoup-worker) is spawned by the Node.js process — requires build dependencies
SFU: each client connection is a separate transport; no automatic routing — developer manages
WebRTC signaling (SDP/ICE exchange) must be implemented separately — use WebSocket or HTTP
STUN/TURN server required for NAT traversal — mediasoup handles media but not STUN/TURN
Horizontal scaling requires custom inter-server communication (mediasoup itself is single-process)

security_score_components.security_notes: DTLS/SRTP media encryption automatic (WebRTC standard). ISC open source — auditable. User-controlled data residency. No vendor tracking. Application-level auth (developer responsibility). Spanish developers (no corporate data collection). WebRTC mandates DTLS encryption — media is always encrypted. User responsible for signaling server security.
